name: next_entry_base_off_by_one
code:
proof fn phi_3_next_entry_base_off_by_one(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.next_entry_base(layer, base, idx) == base + idx * arch.entry_size(layer) + arch.entry_size(layer),
{
}
reason: `next_entry_base` computes `base + (idx+1)*entry_size` which equals `entry_base(idx) + entry_size` — it returns the END of the idx-th entry, not the START of the (idx+1)-th, but these are the same value, potentially confusing callers about semantics

name: next_entry_base_at_max_idx
code:
proof fn phi_4_next_entry_base_at_max_idx(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.next_entry_base(layer, base, (arch.num_entries(layer) - 1) as nat) == base + arch.num_entries(layer) * arch.entry_size(layer),
{
}
reason: At the last valid index, `next_entry_base` equals `base + num_entries * entry_size` — the function can produce addresses at the very end of the layer's address space

name: next_entry_base_exceeds_parent_entry
code:
proof fn phi_5_next_entry_base_exceeds_parent_entry(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        layer > 0,
        idx == arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.next_entry_base(layer, base, idx) == base + (arch.num_entries(layer) + 1) * arch.entry_size(layer),
{
}
reason: When `idx == num_entries` (one past valid range), `next_entry_base` produces an address beyond the parent entry's mapped region — no runtime guard prevents this since the precondition only bounds `idx <= X86_NUM_ENTRIES`
